title: Actinotus omnifertilis
taxonomy: Actinotus omnifertilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is native to New South Wales in Australia. The plant is commonly referred to as the 'Shooting Star' due to its unique flower shape. This species is known to thrive in dry to moist environments, typically in open woodlands and rocky outcrops. The classification of Actinotus omnifertilis is further supported by its botanical classification as Actinotus omnifertilis Hook.

properties: Actinotus omnifertilis, also known as the Flannel Flower, is a plant with potential therapeutic properties. preparations: Actinotus omnifertilis, also known as the Flannel Flower, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various purposes. intro: Actinotus omnifertilis, commonly known as Flannel Flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia. The Flannel Flower is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that may aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Actinotus omnifertilis include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Flannel Flower include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
